name: La Sagra Villages Loop
name_original: Cedillo del Condado, Chozas de Canales y Yunclillos – circular cruzando el río Guadarrama
description: Cycle a moderate 32.2-mile (51.8 km) loop through Cedillo del Condado, Chozas de Canales, and Yunclillos, crossing the Guadarrama River.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Cedillo Del Condado are characterized by the predominantly flat and serene landscape of the La Sagra comarca, north of Toledo. This region features extensive cereal fields and accessible rural roads, making it suitable for various fitness levels. The proximity to the Tagus River also offers picturesque paths and diverse scenery. The terrain generally presents manageable routes with minimal elevation changes. Best touring cycling routes around Cedillo Del Condado The most popular touring cycling route is La Sagra Villages Loop, a moderate 32.2 miles (51.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route winds through the flat, agricultural landscape connecting several local villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Villaluenga, Cabañas, and Villaseca de la Sagra Loop, a moderate 19.7 miles (31.7 km) path. It traverses the flat plains, offering views of the surrounding agricultural land. Local touring cyclists also love the Villaluenga de la Sagra, Cabañas de la Sagra y Bargas – circular route, a 24.9 miles (40.0 km) trail leading through cereal fields and rural roads, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Cedillo Del Condado is defined by flat terrain, extensive cereal fields, and rural roads, with some routes offering proximity to the Tagus River. The network provides options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Cedillo Del Condado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 15 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cedillo Del Condado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cedillo Del Condado?
A: There are over 100 touring cycling routes around Cedillo Del Condado. These routes cover a tracked distance of more than 1,470 km, offering a wide variety of options for cyclists of all levels.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Cedillo Del Condado?
A: Yes, Cedillo Del Condado offers nearly 30 easy touring cycling routes. The region's predominantly flat terrain, characterized by extensive cereal fields, makes it ideal for relaxed rides. These routes often feature well-paved surfaces, perfect for those new to touring cycling.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Cedillo Del Condado?
A: The terrain around Cedillo Del Condado is generally flat and serene, dominated by vast cereal fields within the La Sagra comarca. You'll find mostly well-paved surfaces on many routes, though some, particularly those connecting to Toledo, may include dirt and track roads. The proximity to the Tagus River also offers picturesque paths with diverse scenery.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in the area?
A: While many local routes are moderate in length, Cedillo Del Condado provides access to more extensive cycling paths. You can connect to the broader "Ruta de Don Quijote," which traverses the legendary plains of La Mancha, offering longer stages and iconic landmarks like the windmills of Consuegra. Another option is the 52-kilometer "Via Verde de la Jara" with its unique viaducts and tunnels.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while cycling?
A: Beyond the scenic agricultural landscapes, you can explore several local attractions. Consider a route that passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of St. John the Baptist&lt;/a&gt; in Cedillo del Condado, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2402948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Convent of Santa María de la Cruz&lt;/a&gt;. For a historical experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5438442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Illescas&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option for touring cyclists in the area. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38215743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ugena Main Square – Cedillo del Condado Landmarks loop from Cedillo del Condado&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 28.3 km trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022695845/villaluenga-de-la-sagra-cabanas-de-la-sagra-y-bargas-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villaluenga de la Sagra, Cabañas de la Sagra y Bargas – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, covering 40 km through cereal fields.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Cedillo Del Condado?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling, but early spring is particularly beautiful, especially along routes like the "Via Verde de la Jara," which is known for its vibrant fields of flowers. The predominantly flat terrain makes it accessible across many seasons, though summer can be quite warm.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Yes, for more experienced touring cyclists, there are 12 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10374478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Place – Villaluenga de la Sagra loop from Villaluenga-Yuncler&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 58 km and includes more significant elevation changes, offering a greater challenge.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cedillo Del Condado?
A: The touring cycling routes in Cedillo Del Condado are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 15 reviews. Over 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the serene landscapes and the accessibility of the routes through the La Sagra comarca.

Q: Can I find routes that connect Cedillo Del Condado to the city of Toledo?
A: Yes, there are routes that connect Cedillo Del Condado to the monumental city of Toledo. One such route is approximately 32 km long, largely utilizing dirt and track roads across the vast La Mancha plain. This offers a varied cycling experience with a mix of flat sections and descents, suitable for those looking to combine touring cycling with urban exploration.

Q: Are there routes suitable for families with children?
A: The generally flat and serene landscape of Cedillo Del Condado, with its extensive cereal fields and mostly well-paved rural roads, makes many routes suitable for families. Easy routes with minimal elevation changes are ideal for cycling with children, allowing for a relaxed and enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass by water features or rivers?
A: Yes, the municipality's proximity to the Tagus River means you can find picturesque trails and scenic landscapes along its banks. These routes offer a refreshing change of scenery from the open fields and are ideal for contemplation while cycling.
tips: If you are interested in the phenomena of real estate speculation, you can take a ride with your bike through the successive extensions that were made in this agricultural town in the 2000s, when it was intended to turn it into a new "dormitory city" for Madrid. The town went from having 500 to 2,000 homes, many of which were never inhabited, or even completed. The town went through a tough time after the real estate crisis with high unemployment rates and the hangover of this recent history still lingers today.
The town of Onclelos-Yunclillos appears frequently in Mozarabic documents in the years 1189, 1197, 1231, 1234, 1238 and 1240.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular touring bicycle route is approximately 51.8 kilometers long and generally takes about 3 hours to complete. This duration can vary based on your cycling speed and any stops you make along the way.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route, and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as a 'moderate' touring bicycle experience. While it requires good fitness, it is generally considered suitable for most skill levels. Beginners with some cycling experience and a reasonable fitness level should find it manageable, especially given its mostly paved surface.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Cedillo del Condado, Chozas de Canales y Yunclillos route?
faqs.answer: The route is described as 'mostly paved,' offering a smooth cycling experience for the majority of the journey. However, be aware that some segments in the wider Toledo area can include unpaved or more challenging sections. The landscape features riverside views along the Guadarrama River, with diverse natural environments including fields, pastures, wooded areas, and plains that vary from flat to undulating.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary notable feature of this route is its passage across the Guadarrama River. You'll also pass through several towns with their own character. Specific highlights include exploring Cedillo del Condado Landmarks, cycling through the settlement of Chozas de Canales, and seeing the Yunclillos Town Hall and Church.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, you can typically find parking in or near the towns along the route, such as Cedillo del Condado, Chozas de Canales, or Yunclillos. These towns serve as good starting points for this circular trail.

faqs.question: Is the Cedillo del Condado, Chozas de Canales y Yunclillos route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a touring bicycle route primarily on roads and paths, it is generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is accustomed to cycling alongside you or in a trailer. Always ensure your dog is on a leash when in populated areas or near traffic, and be mindful of local regulations regarding pets in public spaces.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The province of Toledo generally experiences hot summers and mild winters. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are often the most pleasant times for cycling, offering comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery. Avoid the peak summer months if you prefer cooler weather.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public touring bicycle route in the province of Toledo.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with several other paths. You might find yourself on sections of 'De Ayuso a Suso por Arcicóllar: hacia el Camino Real de Guadalupe', 'Camino de Yunclillos a Madrid', 'Camino de Illescas', 'Camino de Lominchar a Yuncos', and 'Camino de Cabañas', among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through towns like Cedillo del Condado, Chozas de Canales, and Yunclillos, you will find opportunities for cafes, pubs, and potentially accommodation. It's always a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially if cycling during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it forms a loop. You will start and end in the same general area, making it convenient for parking and planning your trip.
